response.justification: James is a strong senior individual contributor with genuine developer-tooling credentials (test runners, assertion libraries, CI/CD infrastructure, MCP code generators) and some mobile experience, but the role is fundamentally a people-management position and his resume shows no formal people-management or team-leadership experience. There is no evidence of conducting performance reviews, running one-on-ones, owning team roadmaps, or leading organizational change — core requirements for a Level II Manager. He reads as an excellent staff-level IC, not a manager candidate.
strengths: Deep developer-tools experience: authored test runners (Trial), assertion libraries, CI servers, and device-orchestration tooling
15+ years of broad software development across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems
Demonstrated mentorship inclination via workshops, courses, and community teaching
concerns: No formal people-management experience — the role requires at least 2 years managing engineers
No evidence of technical leadership of teams (roadmap ownership, design validation for others, org-level change)
Mobile-platform experience is dated (2015-2016 iOS/Apple TV) and limited relative to a mobile-ecosystem focus

key_factors.factor: No organizational-change or team-leadership track record
